<!--
  Thanks for sending a PR! 🐛
  Fill in what's relevant and delete what isn't — a small PR with a clear
  description beats a big one with a perfect template.
-->

## 🎯 What does this change?

<!-- One or two sentences. What was wrong or missing, and what does this do about it? -->



## 🔗 Related

<!-- "Closes #12", "Refs #34", or "None". -->

Closes #

---

## 🏷️ Type of change

<!-- Tick every box that applies. These map 1:1 to the CHANGELOG headings. -->

- [ ] ✨ **Added** — new feature
- [ ] 🔄 **Changed** — change in existing functionality
- [ ] ⚠️ **Deprecated** — soon-to-be removed feature
- [ ] 🗑️ **Removed** — feature is now gone
- [ ] 🐛 **Fixed** — bug fix
- [ ] 🔒 **Security** — fixes a vulnerability
- [ ] 📚 **Docs / chore** — no runtime change

### 💥 Is this a breaking change?

- [ ] No — safe in a patch/minor release
- [ ] Yes — it changes public API, config keys, or the database schema

<!-- If yes: what breaks, and what does an upgrader have to do? -->



---

## 🧪 How did you test it?

<!--
  Tell us how you convinced yourself this works. "Added a test" is great.
  "Clicked through the panel and here's what I saw" is also great.
-->



## 📸 Screenshots

<!--
  Anything that changes the Filament UI: a before/after pair goes a long way.
  Delete this section for non-visual changes.
-->

| Before | After |
| --- | --- |
|  |  |

---

## ✅ Checklist

- [ ] `composer lint` — code is Pint-clean
- [ ] `composer analyse` — PHPStan passes
- [ ] `composer test` — the suite is green
- [ ] `composer type-coverage` — still at 100%
- [ ] I added or updated tests covering this change
- [ ] I added an entry under `## [Unreleased]` in [CHANGELOG.md](../CHANGELOG.md), under the matching heading above
- [ ] My commits follow [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) (`feat:`, `fix:`, `docs:`, `chore:` …)
- [ ] I updated the README if this changes behaviour, config, or the public API

<!--
  Don't worry if a box or two is unticked — open the PR anyway and say so.
  Maintainers would rather help you over the line than have you not send it. 💛
-->
